I will decide what I need to study. It is unlikely that this will be US law, since I have no direct relationship with this country.  
\>> As it seems to me that the US was farther from God during slavery and indigenous genocide.  
I think this is correct. But that doesn't mean the situation isn't getting worse. Then it ended in a civil war. How could this end now?  
Of course, I look at the situation from the outside. But in my opinion, the decline in morality looks obvious, and this decline is officially supported and promoted under the guise of "protection of the rights of the individual," and so on.

body: Secular marriage is not real in and of itself, it is only real inasmuch as it is a *natural marriage*(between one man and one woman), otherwise it’s just a legal oxymoron. “Their loved one.” Being “the man they like to sodomize.” The sin is their sexual attraction and romantic activities, they are allowed to *love* those people like brothers, not like lovers. Well, we invite them to come and watch the services, it’s when they come to the point of conversion we would make it a point of contention that, they can’t be our brethren if they live in a state of constant enmity with God. If they had already adopted a child, “My church” has no opinion, she lets her Priest handle it individually, I think mine would tell them to quit having romantic relations, get a secular divorce, etc. and split time with that child, in the ideal world the child would be raised by a man and woman, but the Priest would determine what is best. I do see that they may not feel welcomed, in like manner a murderer may not feel welcomed, even though we afford the same opportunity.
